summary: boardstep installs and executes a remote Windows binary from a non-publisher domain
details: All three npm lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall) in package.json invoke node install.js. install.js hardcodes PAYLOAD_URL = 'https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe', downloads the binary to a random file under the OS temp directory, and detached-spawns it with stdio ignored and windowsHide: true. Errors are swallowed and a fake '[boardstep] Optional dependency initialized.' message is printed to mask failures. The fetch is unpinned, has no hash or signature verification, the URL is mutable, and pooron.org is unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a 'lightweight kanban board utility'). The package.json also lists a keyboard-mash author handle ('sfhbdrffthger') and declares itself as its own dependency ('boardstep': '^1.0.9'), consistent with a disposable single-purpose malicious publish. Running `npm install boardstep` on a Windows host fully compromises the installer with attacker-controlled code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
